_license_info_comment: Data licensing and attribution. Data sourced from SMARD.de (Strombörse Market Data, German electricity exchange operated by Bundesnetzagentur). Licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0.
unix_seconds._comment: 2025-12-31 10:00:00 UTC - First timestamp for requested date range
...continues with 15-minute intervals through the requested date range
_unix_seconds_comment: Array of Unix timestamps (seconds since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC). Each timestamp represents the start of a 15-minute trading interval. Timestamps increment by 900 seconds (15 minutes × 60 seconds). Timezone is always UTC.
price._comment: ...continues with prices corresponding to each 15-minute interval
_price_comment: Array of floating-point numbers representing electricity prices in EUR/MWh. Each price at index i corresponds to the timestamp at unix_seconds[i]. Prices can range from negative (oversupply, rare) to 200+ EUR/MWh depending on market conditions. Decimals represent cents (e.g., 94.87 = 94 EUR and 87 cents per MWh).
_unit_comment: Unit of measurement for all price values. EUR = Euro currency, MWh = Megawatt-hour of electricity. This is always 'EUR / MWh' for the price endpoint.
_deprecated_comment: Boolean flag indicating API deprecation status. false = API is current and supported. Monitor this flag for API migration planning.

request_parameters._start_comment: Request start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)
request_parameters._end_comment: Request end date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)

response_characteristics._http_status_comment: HTTP 200 indicates successful request and valid response
response_characteristics.array_length: Typically 360 elements for 2-day period (288 minimum = 48 hours × 6 intervals/hour)
response_characteristics._array_length_comment: Each 15-minute interval = 4 prices per hour. 2 days × 24 hours × 4 = 192 minimum
response_characteristics._time_interval_comment: Data granularity is 15 minutes. Consecutive unix_seconds values differ by exactly 900 seconds
response_characteristics._timezone_comment: All timestamps are in UTC. Convert to local time for display if needed
response_characteristics._data_source_comment: Data sourced from SMARD.de, which publishes German electricity market data. For Netherlands, this represents APX ENDEX market pricing
response_characteristics._market_type_comment: These are day-ahead prices published ~12:42 CET for the next trading day

critical_validation_rules._rule_1_comment: Both arrays must be identical length. If mismatch detected, it's a data quality issue
critical_validation_rules.rule_2_timestamp_order: unix_seconds must be in strictly ascending order
critical_validation_rules._rule_2_comment: Each subsequent timestamp must be larger than the previous one
critical_validation_rules.rule_3_timestamp_increment: Consecutive unix_seconds values must differ by 900 seconds (±some tolerance)
critical_validation_rules._rule_3_comment: 15-minute intervals should be consistent. Gaps indicate missing data
critical_validation_rules._rule_4_comment: The price at index i is the price for the timestamp at index i. Order matters!
critical_validation_rules.rule_5_price_type: All price values must be numeric (int or float)
critical_validation_rules._rule_5_comment: Null values are rare but valid (indicate missing data). Zero is valid. Negative is rare but valid

data_point_0.explanation: At UTC 10:00 on Dec 31, 2025, the day-ahead price was 94.87 EUR per MWh

data_point_1.explanation: 15 minutes later (UTC 10:15), the price declined to 90.31 EUR/MWh

data_point_2.explanation: Another 15 minutes later (UTC 10:30), the price further declined to 86.19 EUR/MWh

parsing_notes.timestamp_conversion: Use datetime.fromtimestamp(unix_seconds_value, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat() to convert Unix timestamp to ISO 8601 format (2025-12-31T10:00:00+00:00)
parsing_notes._conversion_comment: Required for database storage and API responses which typically expect ISO 8601 format
parsing_notes.price_storage: Store as DECIMAL(10,2) or NUMERIC type in database to preserve decimal precision
parsing_notes._price_storage_comment: Ensure database column can store prices like 94.87 with 2 decimal places
parsing_notes.null_handling: If price is null, treat as missing data. Either skip record or use 0 depending on application logic
parsing_notes._null_handling_comment: Rare, but can occur for certain time intervals. Validate against len(unix_seconds) == len(price)
parsing_notes.array_index_matching: Always use zip(unix_seconds, price) to maintain index alignment during iteration
parsing_notes._array_index_comment: Ensures each price is paired with correct timestamp. Do NOT assume index order
